dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has escalated. The smith got his hands on inferior ore while you were out and tried to work it anyway. The forge fire nearly took the workshop.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} pulled him out before the smoke got him. He was furious: not grateful, furious. Said he was close to a breakthrough on {quest_focus} and the interruption cost him hours. His hands are burned and he's already back at the anvil. {quest_ally} found him trying to trade his last tool set for more fuel.
dialogueChunks.outro: If you found the original {quest_item}, bring it now. He's past the point of reason and the next shortcut he takes might be the one that kills him.

playerResponses.text: I have what he needs. Let's hope it's enough to stop the spiral.
Maybe the fire should have been the wake-up call.

dialogueChunks.intro: The alchemist tried the substitutes. The explosion was visible from three settlements away. The tower is still standing, but barely.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found her in the wreckage, mixing another batch with bleeding hands. She wasn't even shaken by the explosion: she was taking notes on what went wrong. {quest_ally} tried to reason with her and she said: 'I'm closer than anyone has ever been to {quest_focus}. You want me to stop now?' The {enemy_type} camp where {quest_item} was taken is still reachable, but the path runs through {location}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {quest_item} before she tries again with something worse. The next experiment might not leave a tower to stand in.

playerResponses.text: I'll bring the real reagents. At least the next attempt won't be suicidal.
She needs to be stopped, not supplied.

dialogueChunks.intro: The collector's daughter came to see him yesterday. First time in two years. She brought her child: his grandchild he's never met.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He barely looked up from his catalogues. {target_npc} says the daughter stood there for ten minutes holding the baby before she started crying. {quest_ally} tried to intervene but the collector just said: 'After I find the last piece of {quest_focus}. Then I'll have time.' The grandchild was asleep on his desk and he moved it aside to make room for a rubbing of an inscription.
dialogueChunks.outro: The keystone artifact was last traced to a ruin near {location}. Find it. Not for him: for the family that's watching him disappear into {quest_focus} one day at a time.

playerResponses.text: That child deserves a grandfather. I'll find the keystone.
What if the keystone isn't there? Will he finally stop?
His family needs to accept that he's made his choice.
